theory.intro: Geometry uses agreed names and marks so a diagram can be described precisely. The order of letters matters for rays and angles.
facts: A **point** is named by one capital letter, such as point $A$.
An **interval** joins two endpoints; interval $AB$ and interval $BA$ name the same object.
A **ray** starts at its first named point and continues through the second, so ray $AB$ and ray $BA$ are usually different.
A **line** extends forever in both directions; line $AB$ and line $BA$ name the same line.
For $\angle ABC$, the middle letter $B$ is the vertex and the arms are rays $BA$ and $BC$.
Matching ticks mark equal intervals; matching arcs mark equal angles.

foundation.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (2,0);\draw[-{Stealth}] (A)--(3,0);\fill (A) circle (2pt) node[below] {$A$};\fill (B) circle (2pt) node[below] {$B$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Identify the geometrical object shown.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\draw (0,0)--(3,0);\fill (0,0) circle (2pt) node[below] {$C$};\fill (3,0) circle (2pt) node[below] {$D$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Name the interval shown.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\coordinate (Q) at (0,0);\coordinate (P) at (2,0);\coordinate (R) at (1.2,1.8);\draw (P)--(Q)--(R);\node[right] at (P) {$P$};\node[below] at (Q) {$Q$};\node[above] at (R) {$R$};\draw (0.55,0) arc[start angle=0,end angle=56,radius=0.55];\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Name the marked angle.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\draw (0,0)--(4,0);\foreach \x/\n in {0/A,2/B,4/C}{\fill (\x,0) circle (2pt) node[below] {$\n$};}\draw (1,-0.13)--(1,0.13);\draw (3,-0.13)--(3,0.13);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
What do the matching single ticks show?
foundation.solution_text: Interval $AB$ is equal in length to interval $BC$.

development.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\draw[{Stealth}-{Stealth}] (-0.5,0)--(3.5,0);\fill (0.5,0) circle (2pt) node[below] {$M$};\fill (2.5,0) circle (2pt) node[below] {$N$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Identify the object and name it.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\draw[-{Stealth}] (1,0)--(4.5,0);\fill (1,0) circle (2pt) node[below] {$S$};\fill (3,0) circle (2pt) node[below] {$T$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Name the ray that starts at $S$ and passes through $T$.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\coordinate (B) at (0,0);\coordinate (A) at (2,0);\coordinate (C) at (-0.7,1.9);\draw (A)--(B)--(C);\node[right] at (A) {$A$};\node[below] at (B) {$B$};\node[above] at (C) {$C$};\draw (0.55,0) arc[start angle=0,end angle=110,radius=0.55];\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Give both valid three-letter names for the marked angle.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\coordinate (B) at (0,0);\coordinate (A) at (2,0);\coordinate (C) at (1.2,1.7);\draw (A)--(B)--(C);\node[right] at (A) {$A$};\node[below] at (B) {$B$};\node[above] at (C) {$C$};\draw (0.5,0) arc[start angle=0,end angle=55,radius=0.5];\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A student names the marked angle $\angle ACB$. Explain the error and give a correct name.
development.solution_text: The vertex $B$ must be the middle letter, so a correct name is $\angle ABC$.

development.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (3,-2);\coordinate (C) at (3,2);\coordinate (D) at (3,0);\draw (A)--(B)--(C)--cycle;\draw (A)--(D);\foreach \p/\pos in {A/left,B/below,C/above,D/right}{\fill (\p) circle (2pt) node[\pos] {$\p$};}\draw (2.87,-1)--(3.13,-1);\draw (2.87,1)--(3.13,1);\draw (0.65,0) arc[start angle=0,end angle=34,radius=0.65];\draw (0.9,-0.62) arc[start angle=-34,end angle=0,radius=1.1];\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
State all the equality information shown by the marks.

mastery.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\draw[-{Stealth}] (0,0)--(5,0);\foreach \x/\n in {0/P,2/Q,4/R}{\fill (\x,0) circle (2pt) node[below] {$\n$};}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A line contains points $P$, $Q$ and $R$ in that order. Name the ray that is the same as ray $PQ$ using $P$ and $R$.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.75]\coordinate (O) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (1.3,0);\coordinate (A) at (3,0);\coordinate (C) at (1.4,2.2);\draw (A)--(O)--(C);\foreach \p/\pos in {O/below,A/right,B/below,C/above}{\fill (\p) circle (2pt) node[\pos] {$\p$};}\draw (0.65,0) arc[start angle=0,end angle=58,radius=0.65];\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
The marked angle has vertex $O$ and arms through $A$ and $C$. Point $B$ lies on arm $OA$. Give four valid three-letter names for the angle.
